rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the intended component type or implementation scope, following AADL architecture rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Use valid component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when inserting annex blocks
Follow AADL scoping rules for annex placement
Preserve existing component structure and connections
Use valid feature names in annex contracts
Avoid mixing component type and implementation-level constructs in the wrong scopes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Follow proper AGREE expression syntax for comparisons and arithmetic
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not mix component type and implementation-level constructs in the wrong scopes
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
